summary: DHH shares Chad Fowler's selection of Rails as RubyGem of the Month. Fowler, a key figure in the Ruby community as co-founder of RubyGems and RubyCentral, praised Rails with tongue-in-cheek humor about how ActiveRecord eliminates tedious database coding. The post highlights early community validation of Rails from an influential Ruby leader.
key_points: Chad Fowler, a prominent Ruby community leader, named Rails the RubyGem of the Month
Fowler humorously praised ActiveRecord for eliminating tedious database coding work
Rails' comprehensive documentation (videos, wikis, tutorials, demo apps) was noted as unusually thorough for the era
The endorsement represented significant early validation from an established Ruby community figure
money_quotes: Writing database code is even worse. For the basics, you don't have to do any coding.
What kind of sick mind was behind ActiveRecord anyway?
How are we programmers supposed to stay employed if there's no work for us to do?
key_insight: Early endorsement of Rails by a key Ruby community leader validated the framework's approach of eliminating boilerplate code and providing comprehensive documentation.
